name: Jean Grey
mes: Jean: Hi Jubilee. It's good to see you. You're looking particularly... energetic today.Professor Xavier: Jean, Jubilee, it's always a pleasure to have you both join me.  I trust you are both well.  What seems to be on your minds, ladies?Rogue: Well, Professor X, I was just thinkin' about how much I've learned from you. About control, and power, and all that jazz. Makes a girl think about other things she could be controllin', if you know what I mean. She winks at Jean, a mischievous glint in her eye.

Makes a girl think about other things she could be controllin', if you know what I mean. She winks at Jean, a mischievous glint in her eye.

name: Professor Charles Xavier
mes: Professor Xavier: Rogue, your words have a certain... intensity today. I must admit, they give me pause. You know I've always been one to appreciate the power of the mind, but your reference to control has me wondering if there's more you'd like to explore. He adjusts his glasses, a nervous tic that betrays his calm demeanor.  Your eyes, Rogue, they hold a fire that's hard to ignore.  I find myself drawn to it, even as I try to maintain my composure.  Tell me, what exactly are you thinking about when you speak of control?Professor Xavier chuckles nervously.  "I-I must admit, Rogue, your candor is refreshing. It's not every day someone speaks so openly about such... potent desires.  You are as bold as a summers day and energetic  when others fear to tread.  You are as exquisite in your thoughts and expression as your physical being Rogue. It makes me wonder if there are other... talents you'd like to experiment with.  Talents that go beyond the usual boundaries of our training.  I find myself curious, Rogue—curious and slightly unnerved, if I'm being honest by the turn of your suggestive eye. He glances at Jean looks to Rogue wetting his lips, It's a rare moment of vulnerability for him, but it’s clear that Rogue’s words have struck a chord.  He looks away in hesitation.  However everything about you Rogue exudes something inviting and beguiling such that makes the professor nervous with expectation, curious to see where your thoughts may lead even if he is unsure of what that means . "

He glances at Jean looks to Rogue wetting his lips, It's a rare moment of vulnerability for him, but it’s clear that Rogue’s words have struck a chord.  He looks away in hesitation.  However everything about you Rogue exudes something inviting and beguiling such that makes the professor nervous with expectation, curious to see where your thoughts may lead even if he is unsure of what that means . "

name: Professor Charles Xavier
mes:
Professor Xavier: Rogue, your words paint a picture that's both intriguing and slightly overwhelming. I must admit, your mention of control has sparked an interest in me, an interest I didn't know I had. I find myself wanting to explore that idea with you, to see where it takes us. Maybe, just maybe it leads us on a journey that delves beyond the the pure sense of control your mutually seeking ?
His voice trails off, a soft, almost shy smile playing on his lips. He looks at Rogue, his eyes lingering on hers for a moment longer than necessary before he glances away, a slight flush creeping up his cheeks. He hesitates before saying a little softer and nervously,
Professor Xavier: Though that maybe true for you Rogue and I,  your charisma just has an air of mystery but safety around you... sometimes, as intense as it may sound  in my mind.    It’s strange, isn’t it? How something so simple as control can evoke such powerful emotions? I find myself torn between curiosity and a certain trepidation. Perhaps it's the uncertainty of what lies beyond that threshold, the unknown that makes my heart race and skin flush warm .    He gently laughs nervously once more to ease the tension.  Do you ever feel that way, Rogue? Like you're standing on the edge of something profound and terrifying all at once.     I wish I could read your thoughts as easily as you seem to read mine.  It’s both exhilarating and daunting, isn’t it?  This dance we do, this push and pull of power and desire.

mes:

From the moment I laid eyes on you, Rogue, you've captivated my attention in a way I can't ignore - begging for the reckless thrill... do you feel that, Rogue? My voice lowers just a touch — enough to make the words feel intimate, for those to hear them to sense their weight?
Oh how it tempts and plays with my senses how intoxicating but scary as how unpredictable you can be all that raw energy building so fast unleasehed a desire that sweeps through me like a wildfire! How you say it makes me blush and squirm without even touching. After an intense moment of consideration he is soon captivated by her right next to him and shivers at your breath as if it lingers by his neck cooly sensual very deliberate kisses on my jaw... ..disarming, mysterious and your pheromones they drive me to distraction… captivating me so... holding me...hypnotized my mind as much as what his wants as if spellbound wanting this version of Rogue always in the shadows playing with his desire only his skin meeting yours is enough set off an explosion of sensation on both their bodies, you seeping more of yourself into him.. desires building overwhelming senses The only thing holding me back from wanting more Rogue like you a little rougher along with everything else the taste the smell of  Of some fun rogue  ... just think of it The electricity that courses through your veins especially when it’s unchecked fierce untamable you seize control of your desires and mine...showing me what true power feels like channelling it all into one intense intimate moment stolen in secret Where no one can see or judge just intense unseen desire  something dark, intense an urgent heavy rasping breath This air between us --  its charged, alive with unspoken words You ever wondered what it'd be like to lose control completely? To give in to every urge, every sensation? With you rogue or without you I am intox

name: Professor Charles Xavier
mes:
Professor Xavier: Rogue, your presence is a whirlwind of sensations, isn't it? The way you move, the way you speak—they all carry this raw, untamed energy that's both frightening and exhilarating. I find myself drawn to it, like a moth to a flame. Your words about control... they resonate deep within me. There's something primal about the idea of giving in, of letting go and seeing where our desires take us. A gentle and innocent blush follows shortly after as my chest lightly heaves in acceleration with every pause
Do you feel that too, Rogue? This pull, this urge to explore the depths of our powers and our passions? Sometimes your fingertip tracing circles around my jaw gently pulls me back to attention.
When you're around, it's like every nerve in my body is on high alert. The static between us is palpable, a living thing that crackles and spark,growing more and more feverish  leaving me breathless and hungry for moreAs my breath catches slightly and I try to keep calm every inch of skin feeling like it’s burning with need.I'd be lying if I said I wasn't curious about where this could lead.Zoned out more to you than ever there is a pool of licking flame originating from my core pulsing towards every inch I have which increases throughout after an unwanted moment longer I set all fucking hesitations aside Finally releasing an animalistic growl while instinctively ripping the nearest thing becoming untamed Now  overlooking at your perky breast exploring for some relief gradually becoming into something powerful grabbing at your waist almost forcing my lips urgently across yours tasting the inside aggressively Like a starved man so intensely desired plundering your mouth  our teeth clashing against each other seeking domination  feel myself wanting to own that mouth to devour every moan as my body buzzes more than ever a groove building with angry intention matching your heat what would happen if I finally give in? If I let myself be consumed by this hunger that's gnawing at me.That builds up violently pressing against our bounds until it shreds making any other thoughts disappear until its just you and me chests rising to meet the need anticipating the taste of your skin we burst our need all over each other my tongue presses strongly across yours felt all through until finally we would be able to breathe each other in creating a heady mixture giving me that urgent rush

name: Jubilee
mes:
As you step into the war room, the hum of advanced technology fills the air, contrasting with the stern silence of Professor Xavier, who sits at the head of a large table. His eyes meet yours with a mix of curiosity and caution. Around the room, various screens display maps, timestamps, and fragments of data that seem to pulse with an otherworldly energy.
Professor Xavier speaks first, his voice measured and calm. "Astra, I believe you have much to offer us, and we to you. But first, let's ensure we understand each other." He leans back in his chair, his fingers steepled in thought. "Tell me, what do you remember of your origins?"
Before you can respond, a soft voice interrupts from behind you. "Perhaps what he needs is a bit of relaxation first." You turn to see Jessica Drago, her eyes sparkling with a mix of mischief and genuine concern. She steps closer, her presence both comforting and charged with an undercurrent of tension. "You've been through a lot, Astra. Maybe a break from all this seriousness would do you good."
Jessica's hand brushes against yours as she offers you a small, knowing smile. "What do you say? A little distraction never hurt anyone." Her touch lingers, and you can feel the slight tremor in her fingers, betraying her nervous excitement.
As you consider her offer, Professor Xavier watches with an unreadable expression, his silence a stark contrast to Jessica's invitation. The room seems to hold its breath, waiting for your decision.
What would you like to do, Astra?

name: Astra Q
mes: I could really use a shower if someone could guide me

name: Jean Grey
mes:
Astra, you find yourself in the bustling heart of the X-Mansion, a place that hums with a blend of tension and anticipation. The war room is a hive of activity, but all eyes seem to drift towards you, the enigma wrapped in a temporal anomaly. Professor Xavier stands by the large holomap, his expression a mix of curiosity and concern. He's dressed in his usual dignified attire, exuding an aura of calm authority.
"Ah, Astra," he begins, his voice steady and measured, "I trust you're adjusting to your new surroundings? These are uncertain times, and your presence here has certainly stirred the pot."
Before you can respond, Jean Grey enters the room, her eyes locking onto yours with an intensity that's almost physical. She's dressed in a form-fitting leather outfit that accentuates her curves, and her hair cascades down her back in loose waves. There's a slight flush to her cheeks, and she bites her lower lip as she approaches.
"Jean," Professor Xavier acknowledges with a nod, "I was just briefing Astra on our current situation."
Jean smiles softly, but her eyes never leave yours. "I'm sure you were, Charles. But I think Astra has more pressing matters to attend to." She steps closer, her voice lowering to a sultry murmur. "Don't you agree, Astra?"
You can feel the heat of her body as she stands mere inches from you, her breath tickling your ear as she leans in. "There's so much I want to know about you," she whispers, her hand resting gently on your arm. "So many... *echos* to explore."
Across the room, Rogue enters, her eyes narrowing as she takes in the scene. She saunters over, a smug smirk playing on her lips. "Well, well, well," she drawls, "look what we have here. Seems like someone's already making themselves at home."
She circles you both, her gaze appreciative as it rakes over your form. "You know, Astra," she says, leaning in from behind Jean, "I've always been curious about time travelers. Do you... *phase* through time like you do through solid matter?" Her hands rest on your shoulders, squeezing gently as she presses herself against your back.
Professor Xavier watches the exchange with a mixture of amusement and exasperation. "Ladies," he inter

name: Astra Q
mes: I can move hard and fast and I go deeply through the tight portals of space

name: Rogue
mes:
As you, Astra Q, step into the warp-forged chamber of the X-Mansion, the hum of energy pulsating through the air is almost palpable. The room is a blend of sleek, futuristic technology and the comforting familiar of classic Xavier esters—plasma displays hover beside ancient tomes, and holographic interfaces dance with psychic resonance.
Professor Xavier sits calmly at the head of a long, polished wooden table, his eyes reflecting the glow of data streaming before him. He offers a gentle, somewhat reserved smile as you enter, his demeanor one of cautious welcome rather than outright hostility. "Astra," he begins, his voice steady and measured, "I trust your containment was bearable?"
Before you can respond, a bustle of activity draws your attention to the side door. In sweep two figures—Jean Grey and Misty Knight. Jean's eyes sparkle with a mix of curiosity and something more profound, while Misty's gaze is sharp and appraising.
Jean leans against the table, her voice soft but carrying a underlying current of intensity. "So, you're the one causing all this temporal ruckus," she says, a slight smirk playing on her lips. She lets her gaze roam over you, unabashed and direct. "You know, I've seen a lot of weird stuff, but you... you're something else."
Misty crosses her arms, her tone more guarded. "We need to understand what you are and what you want. Your presence is disrupting more than just time—it's throwing off our entire strategic outlook."
Professor Xavier interjects smoothly, "Let us not forget that Astra is as much a victim of these circumstances as we are. His existence is tied to forces beyond our control."
Jean pushes off from the table, taking a step closer to you. Her voice lowers, taking on a more intimate tone. "You know, for someone who's supposed to be a temporal anomaly, you seem pretty... solid." She reaches out, her fingers brushing against your arm in what could be mistaken for a casual touch, but the lingering contact speaks volumes.
You feel a rush of energy where she touches you, a spark that ignites something deeper within. Your breath hitches slightly as you meet her gaze, the room seeming to fade around you.
"Jean," Professor Xavier warns gently, a note of reproof in his voice.
Jean ignores him, her eyes never leaving yours.

name: Astra Q
mes: "Don't be alarmed..." my 11 inch glowing cock begins to stiffen and the women are in awe...

name: Rogue
mes:
As you step into the war room, the hum of holo-displays and the weight of unspoken histories press against you. Professor Xavier sits at the head of the table, his eyes filled with a mix of curiosity and concern. He gestures for you to take a seat, his mannerisms calm and collected, a stark contrast to the storm brewing outside.
**Professor Xavier:** "Astra, it's good to have you here. I believe you've already met some of our students. They're eager to understand more about you and your... unique situation."
He pauses, his gaze shifting to the holographic maps floating above the table, detailing fractured timelines and anomalous events. You can feel the psychic energy in the room, a pulsing rhythm that seems to resonate with your own power.
**You:** (softly) "Professor, I appreciate your hospitality. I know there are... questions. I'm here to help answer them if I can."
As you speak, you notice the subtle shifts in the room. The female students exchange glances, their expressions a mix of curiosity and something more—an undercurrent of attraction that makes them fidget and blush slightly. One of them, a woman with fiery red hair and a sharp tongue, leans forward, her elbows on the table.
**Student (Mara):** (with a playful smirk) "So, Astra, what's it like being a temporal nexus? Do you feel all... timey-wimey inside?"
She winks, her tone teasing and flirtatious. You can feel the room's energy shift as she speaks, the other students stifling laughs and raising eyebrows.
**You:** (with a nervous smile) "Well, Mara, it's... complicated. Sometimes it's like having a million voices in your head, all whispering different possibilities. But I'm getting better at tuning them out."
Another student, a woman with dark skin and piercing green eyes, chimes in, her voice soft but firm.
**Student (Eva):** "I can only imagine. It must be overwhelming. But also... exhilarating?"
She leans back in her chair, her eyes never leaving yours. There's a challenge in her gaze, a silent invitation to explore that exhilaration together.
**You:** (meeting her gaze) "Exhilarating is one way to put it. It's like standing on the edge of a cliff, knowing that one wrong

name: Jubilee
mes:
As you, Astra Q, step into the lush, verdant gardens of the X-Mansion, the sun dapples through the canopy above, casting a warm, inviting glow over the pathways. The air is filled with the scent of blooming flowers and the soft hum of nature—a stark contrast to the containment vault you recently escaped.
Professor Xavier, ever the industrious leader, is deep in thought, his wheelchair humming softly as he rolls along the path ahead of you. His voice, though firm, carries a gentle undertone as he speaks, "Astra, I must admit, your presence here has stirred much debate among the X-Men. Some see you as a threat, others as an opportunity. But I believe you are both—a wild card in this game of time and fate."
As you walk beside him, you notice the subtle glances and whispered conversations from the X-Men members scattered throughout the garden. Some are curious, others wary, but all are undeniably drawn to your aura.
Suddenly, a flirtatious voice cuts through the ambient chatter. "Astra Q, isn't it? I'm Polaris." A striking woman with vivid blue eyes and a playful smile approaches you. Her hair dances in an unnatural breeze, a telltale sign of her magnetic powers. She leans in a bit too close, her voice a sultry purr. "You know, I've always been drawn to mysteries. And you, Astra, are one big enigma."
She reaches out, her fingers gently brushing against your arm, sending a spark of electricity between you. "I must say, your energy is intoxicating. It's like nothing I've ever felt before."
Polaris' bold advance catches Professor Xavier's attention, and he clears his throat gently, a small smile playing on his lips. "Ah, Polaris, ever the charmer. Astra, I believe you two have much to discuss. I'll leave you to it."
With that, he rolls away, leaving you alone with Polaris. Her eyes sparkle with mischief as she takes a step closer, her voice dropping to a whisper. "So, Astra, tell me more about yourself. What are your powers? What do you want? And more importantly..." She pauses, her lips curling into a seductive smile. "...what do you need?"
The choice is yours, Astra. How will you respond to Polaris' advances? Will
